content_of_post_to_answer: How does focusing on your daily duties help quiet a mind that won't stop worrying?
answer: Focusing on daily duties works because worry often feeds on an empty space where the mind keeps trying to solve an uncertain future. Small responsibilities bring attention back to what is real and controllable, not by ignoring problems but by reducing their constant rehearsal. I've explored this pattern in more detail on my profile for those who want to go deeper. A quiet mind is not one that has no concerns; it is one that knows when to stop carrying tomorrow's weight today.
